crime.value: Death of Eliza's husband, circumstances unknown
crime.quote: I received a phone call from Eliza Browne, announcing that her husband had been found dead.

victim.value: Eliza Browne's husband, reasons for targeting unknown
victim.quote: her husband had been found dead

closed_world.value: Small Appalachian mountain town with only five houses on the peak
closed_world.quote: There were a total of five houses on the particular peak I lived on, one being my own.

investigator.value: Kenzie Stone, journalist and narrator who receives the death announcement
investigator.quote: as a journalist, I spent a lot of time on my laptop at home typing

suspect_architecture.value: Small mountain community with five houses - neighbors Eliza Browne (pregnant wife of victim), Benji (dinner companion with alibi timing), Agatha (apple-giver), plus isolated narrator as potential suspect
suspect_architecture.quote: There were a total of five houses on the particular peak I lived on

red_herring.value: Apple allergy detail - narrator's specific allergy to apples from Agatha's basket may misdirect from real murder method
red_herring.quote: I can't, I sighed. The apples looked nearly perfect. I'm allergic.

clue_fairness.value: Precise timing established - narrator's whereabouts with Benji until late, exact time of death call at 2:13 AM creates verifiable timeline
clue_fairness.quote: At exactly 2:13 a.m., I received a phone call from Eliza Browne

social_world.value: Isolated mountain community with informal social bonds - inherited property, neighborly exchanges, journalist outsider status
social_world.quote: I lived alone in the house that my Great Aunt Ida had left for me to inherit

alibi.value: Narrator has dinner alibi with Benji, but timing creates opportunity window - left late, alone when death call came
alibi.quote: I think I should probably get going, I did eventually say

investigator_wound.value: Narrator's isolation and father loss - lived alone by choice, father died when 13, closer to Great Aunt than family
investigator_wound.quote: Her death affected me more greatly than my father's, who passed away when I was 13
